_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Pregnancy complications requiring Dilation and Evacuation

preauth.label: Detailed clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, indications, and advice for admission
Ultrasound report for establishing the diagnosis

claim.label: Histopathology report of evacuated tissue (optional), however in certain conditions such as molar pregnancy or any other suspected pathology it should be available

ppd_preauth.q: Are detailed Clinical notes (vitals, history, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication, planned line of treatment, and advice for admission) complete?
Does the Ultrasonography report provide gestation period / condition of the pregnancy and confirm the diagnosis?
Did the ultrasound findings justify the need for the procedure?
Is the uterine size below 20 weeks?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details provided?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the imaging indicative of procedure?
Was Serum HCG levels report in hydatidiform mole etiology submitted?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge submitted?

common_queries: Ultrasound findings do not justify the need for the procedure
Uterine size is 20 weeks or above
Detailed clinical notes (history, symptoms, signs, examination, indication, planned treatment, advice for admission) are incomplete or missing
Ultrasound report does not provide gestation period or confirm diagnosis
Serum HCG levels report not submitted for hydatidiform mole etiology
Histopathology report for evacuated tissue not provided for molar pregnancy or other suspected pathology
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and treatment details are missing
Discharge summary with follow-up advice is missing

clinical_key_pointers: Dilation and evacuation (D&E) is the most common procedure used to perform first trimester and second trimester abortions.
It is performed between 6-20 weeks of development.
Dilatation and Evacuation procedure is performed as a two-step process: Cervical dilatation and Evacuation of uterine content.
Uterine evacuation between 16 – 20 weeks uterine size should be done preferably under Ultrasound guidance.
common_clinical_presentation: Passage of vesicles in case of hydatidiform mole
